name: Carvana Auto Finance
description_short: Online used car dealer with in-house financing. Accepts credit scores as low as 500. Delivery to your door.
description_long:
Carvana is an online used car retailer that offers in-house financing, making it a popular option for subprime borrowers who may struggle to get approved at traditional dealerships. Founded in 2012 in Phoenix, Arizona, Carvana has grown to become one of the largest online auto dealers in the US.

Carvana accepts credit scores as low as 500 for financing, though rates vary significantly based on creditworthiness. Buyers can browse inventory online, get pre-qualified without affecting their credit score, and have the vehicle delivered to their door or pick it up from a Carvana vending machine.

Every Carvana vehicle comes with a 7-day money-back guarantee (up to 400 miles), a free 100-day/4,189-mile limited warranty, and a 150-point inspection. Financing terms range from 36-72 months with rates starting around 6.9% APR for well-qualified buyers but can exceed 20% for subprime borrowers.

Carvana also accepts trade-ins (with an online instant offer) and allows cash purchases. The entirely online process eliminates the traditional dealership negotiation experience, which many buyers prefer.
pros: Accepts credit scores as low as 500
cons: Higher APR for subprime borrowers (can exceed 20%)
Can't test drive before buying (7-day return mitigates this)
Vehicle condition may vary despite inspections
best_for: Buying a car with bad credit (500+ score)
People who dislike traditional car dealership experience
Convenient vehicle delivery without leaving home
diagnosis: Carvana is an online used car marketplace and auto lender specializing exclusively in vehicle purchase financing. It does not offer personal loans and does not fit any personal lending category in this directory.
